## Summary
- Add optional bundled Workboard plugin with gateway card CRUD methods and persisted Kanban-style card state.
- Add Control UI Workboard tab for filtering, creating, moving, deleting, and starting session handoff from cards.
- Add explicit Workboard card execution actions for Codex and Claude sessions while keeping the existing default Start path model-agnostic.
- Add Workboard docs, plugin inventory/reference metadata, runtime sidecar metadata, labels, changelog, and generated i18n.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo && git diff --check`

- Prior PR proof retained: focused Workboard UI/plugin tests, i18n/docs/generated metadata checks, `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: optional Workboard plugin can back a dashboard Kanban-style board, persist cards, link planned agent work to sessions/runs/tasks, and explicitly start/open Codex or Claude sessions from cards.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS; remote manual environment setup requested after this PR push.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo && git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
Evidence after fix: typecheck/diff command exited 0; autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
Observed result after fix: explicit Codex/Claude executions persist engine/model/session metadata; default starts clear stale explicit execution metadata; transient missing sessions do not mark executions blocked.
What was not tested: local Workboard Vitest reruns after the latest patch stalled in Rolldown transform before test results; manual browser testing via Crabbox/WebVNC is being set up.

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

target.title: fix(sessions): honor explicit default model pins
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist explicit user selections of the configured default model as exact session model pins instead of clearing them as if they were unpinned defaults.
- Keep `/model default`, `session_status(model=default)`, and `sessions.patch model:null` as the explicit clear/unpin paths.
- Carry the exact selection through reply, queued-run, agent-command, reset, status, and sessions.patch paths so channel/parent/config fallback policy cannot silently replace user intent.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Explicit `/model <configured default>` selections no longer lose user intent just because they match the configured primary; fallback policy treats them as strict user session selections, while `/model default` still clears back to unpinned behavior.
Real environment tested: Local beatrice.local source checkout at PR head `0ba67184c403941421ac9e409b592829dd0c224c` on May 23, 2026. Built with `node scripts/build-all.mjs`, then ran a local behavior probe against the built `dist/` session/status modules. No channel credentials or provider API keys were used.

target.title: perf: reduce gateway benchmark filesystem churn
target.body:
## Summary

This PR applies the findings from the gateway CPU benchmark profile review. Across 482 gateway watch profiles, sampled time was mostly idle, but active samples clustered around filesystem and path work: repeated `lstat`/`open`/`stat`/`realpathSync`, catalog JSON reads, bundled-channel boundary checks, and unbounded profile artifact growth.

Changes:

- Channel catalog reads now use a process-local cache for mutable external catalog files. This keeps external catalog freshness tied to process-stable gateway metadata while avoiding repeated JSON reads when hot paths ask for the same catalog repeatedly.
- Channel plugin catalog calls can now receive already-known plugin install records or discovery results. This keeps callers able to carry prepared facts into catalog resolution instead of forcing another installed-index/discovery pass.
- Bundled channel module boundary roots are memoized in a bounded LRU map. Generated bundled entries repeatedly resolve the same package root, plugin directory, module path, and channel metadata; this avoids redoing the same `isPathInside` path checks for stable runtime module loads.
- Gateway watch benchmark mode now sets a default CPU profile retention cap of 40 files, while preserving explicit `OPENCLAW_RUN_NODE_CPU_PROF_MAX_FILES` overrides.
- `scripts/run-node.mjs` rotates old per-command `.cpuprofile` artifacts before starting a new profiled child. Rotation is command-scoped, best-effort, and disabled unless a positive retention cap is set.
- The changelog records the gateway/perf behavior so the benchmark artifact and hot-path changes are visible in release notes.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/infra/gateway-watch-tmux.test.ts src/infra/run-node.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugins-core.catalog.paths.contract.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugins-core.catalog.entries.contract.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway benchmark profiles showed active CPU concentrated in repeated filesystem/path/catalog work, while benchmark mode accumulated hundreds of large `.cpuprofile` artifacts.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node/pnpm repo build and Vitest lanes.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/infra/gateway-watch-tmux.test.ts src/infra/run-node.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugins-core.catalog.paths.contract.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugins-core.catalog.entries.contract.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; `git diff --check`; autoreview commit review.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed: 3 Vitest shards, 86 tests. Full build passed through tsdown, runtime postbuild, plugin SDK DTS generation, export checks, asset copy, build-info, startup metadata, and CLI compat generation. Autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Catalog file reads are process-local cached, bundled channel boundary resolution is reused per stable module path, and gateway watch benchmark runs pass a default profile retention cap into the child runner.

What was not tested: I did not start a live gateway watch benchmark and collect a new long-running CPU profile corpus in this PR; the implementation is covered by focused unit/contract tests and build proof.

target.title: refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- rename the former Pi agent runtime/package surfaces to OpenClaw-owned agent runtime names
- internalize the runtime packages into core and wire the embedded OpenClaw runtime through the current plugin/runtime contracts
- remove copied session-export HTML assets, package-manager install/update leftovers, runtime `.pi` auth discovery, and stale internal Pi naming
- split modern agent runtime id helpers from deprecated Pi runtime-id compatibility; keep compatibility in explicit doctor/session/env/plugin-boundary helpers
- keep deprecated plugin SDK compatibility only at named boundaries, including `resolveLegacyAgentAccessToken`
- add the Pi third-party notice for incorporated code

## Verification

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout plus Linux Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/aliases.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/backend.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>`; `git diff --check`; `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ... corepack pnpm check:changed`.

target.title: Retry node shell fallback on ENOENT
target.body:
## Summary

- Retry node-host `system.run` shell execution with trusted absolute `/usr/bin/sh` when the default `/bin/sh` spawn fails with ENOENT.
- Keep PATH-resolved shell fallback out of the retry chain so execution does not switch to an inherited-PATH binary after approval analysis.
- Keep non-shell and Windows invocations unchanged.
- Add regression coverage for the runtime retry chain.

Fixes #85202

## Why

Linux headless node hosts can report `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT` even when the node is connected and `system.which`/manual host checks show a usable shell at `/usr/bin/sh`. The previous node-host path treated the first spawn error as terminal, so `exec host=node` failed before trying another trusted POSIX shell path.

## Verification

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts src/infra/node-shell.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.sanitize-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`
  - 3 Vitest shards passed; 24 tests
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Node-host `system.run` no longer treats a missing default `/bin/sh` spawn as the only possible POSIX shell path; the patched node host retries trusted absolute `/usr/bin/sh` on ENOENT.

Real environment tested: Linux VPS source checkout at `/root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback`, Node.js v22.22.2, patched OpenClaw node-host runtime code at PR head `fd0fdfb445`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts src/infra/node-shell.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.sanitize-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts && pnpm tsgo:core:test && git diff --check` from the patched checkout. The regression imports the actual node-host `runCommand` implementation and simulates `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT`, then successful `/usr/bin/sh` execution. It no longer includes a PATH-resolved `sh` fallback.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the live command on the patched checkout:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  13 passed (13)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  2 passed (2)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 19.64s
```

Observed result after fix: The node-host retry path continued after `/bin/sh` ENOENT and returned success from `/usr/bin/sh` with stdout `NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK`. The retry sequence asserted by the patched runtime regression is:

```text
/bin/sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
/usr/bin/sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
```

What was not tested: I do not have the reporter's exact Linux headless node/systemd setup, so I could not reproduce their original `/bin/sh` ENOENT on a connected remote node before patch. The PR keeps the fallback narrow and should still benefit from reporter or maintainer live validation on the exact #85202 environment.

target.title: fix(agents): add .catch() to fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises
target.body:
## Problem

Three fire-and-forget promises in `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` can reject without a `.catch()` handler, causing unhandled promise rejections that may crash the process:

1. **`schedulePendingLifecycleError`** (line 345): `void completeSubagentRun({...})` without `.catch()`.
2. **`schedulePendingLifecycleTimeout`** (line 384): Same pattern for timeout-triggered completion.
3. **`ensureListener` event handler** (line 1022): `completeSubagentRun({...})` inside an async IIFE without `.catch()`.

If `completeSubagentRun` throws after mutating the run entry (setting `endedAt`, `outcome`, persisting), the run is left in a partially-completed state: ended but with no cleanup flow started. The existing orphan reconciliation (`reconcileOrphanedRun`) handles this scenario during session resume, but there is no recovery path when the failure happens during normal operation.

## Fix

1. **Add `.catch()` to all three fire-and-forget paths** with `log.warn` for diagnostics.
2. **Schedule two-path catch recovery** in each catch handler via `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId)`. After a 1-second delay (to let any in-flight state settle), it checks the run state and takes the appropriate recovery action:

   - **Path 1 (orphan):** If the child session is gone (`resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` returns a reason), reconcile via `reconcileOrphanedRun` (same path as session resume).
   - **Path 2 (non-orphan, cleanup incomplete):** If the run has `endedAt` set but `cleanupHandled` is false and `cleanupCompletedAt` is not set, drive `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` directly. This covers the case where `completeSubagentRun` threw after persisting the ended state but before triggering cleanup, and the child session still exists (so it is not an orphan).
   - **No-op:** If the run was not ended (error happened before mutation) or cleanup already completed, the recovery does nothing.

Production diff is +46 lines in `subagent-registry.ts`, plus 163 lines of test coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Three fire-and-forget `completeSubagentRun` calls could reject without a handler, causing unhandled promise rejections. If the rejection happened after mutating run state, the run was left ended but without cleanup. The fix adds `.catch()` with orphan recovery to all three paths.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 2fe201d) at `/tmp/fix-84616`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
Step 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-84616
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

Step 2. Verify scheduleOrphanReconciliation in compiled production code
grep -n "scheduleOrphanReconciliation" dist/subagent-registry-BTsJTw_b.js

Step 3. Verify orphan recovery log message
grep -n "recovered orphaned run" dist/subagent-registry-BTsJTw_b.js

Step 4. Start the patched gateway
timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway

Step 5. Run subagent registry test suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**Compiled production code verification**

The `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` function and all three call sites are present in the compiled bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1713:function scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId) {
1763:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
1796:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
2238:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(evt.runId);
```

Both recovery paths are present in the compiled function:

```console
Path 1: orphan recovery
$ grep -n "recovered orphaned run" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1728:log.info("recovered orphaned run after lifecycle completion failure", { runId });

Path 2: non-orphan cleanup resume
$ grep -n "resumed cleanup for ended" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1733:if (startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow(runId, entry)) log.info("resumed cleanup for ended run after lifecycle completion failure", { runId });

Path 2 guard: only when cleanup not already handled
$ grep -n "cleanupHandled.*cleanupCompletedAt" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js | grep 1732
1732:if (!entry.cleanupHandled && typeof entry.cleanupCompletedAt !== "number") {
```

Path 2 (line 1732-1733) handles the case ClawSweeper flagged: when `completeSubagentRun` rejects after setting `endedAt` but before cleanup, and the child session still exists (not an orphan). It calls `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` to resume the cleanup flow that was interrupted.

**Live gateway startup proof**

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T14:52:15.351-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T14:52:15.405-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T14:52:17.667-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T14:52:18.618-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T14:52:18.620-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.2s)
2026-05-21T14:52:19.121-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T14:52:24.916-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T14:52:24.969-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 45ms
```

**Vitest test suite (64 tests)**

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (32 tests) 2729ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (32 tests) 2544ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
```

**Fault injection proof: .catch() + scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery fires in real gateway**

The `.catch()` handler was triggered in a **live running gateway** by injecting a conditional throw into `completeSubagentRun` in `dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js` (line 919) and a timed trigger that simulates a lifecycle completion failure. The injection calls the real `log.warn` and `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` functions through the module's subsystem logger.

**Steps:**
```bash
Step 1. Back up compiled module
cp dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js.bak

Step 2. Inject conditional throw at start of completeSubagentRun (line 919)
sed -i 's/const completeSubagentRun = async (completeParams) => {/const completeSubagentRun = async (completeParams) => { if (completeParams._injectedProof) throw new Error("INJECTED: subagent lifecycle completion failure");/' dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js

Step 3. Append timed trigger at end of module (fires 5s after load)
cat >> dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js << 'EOF'
setTimeout(() => {
  const fakeRunId = "INJECTED-PROOF-RUN";
  completeSubagentRun({
    _injectedProof: true, runId: fakeRunId, endedAt: Date.now(),
    outcome: { status: "error", error: "injected proof" },
    reason: "error", sendFarewell: false, triggerCleanup: true
  }).catch((err) => {
    log.warn("lifecycle error completion failed", { err: err.message, runId: fakeRunId });
    scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(fakeRunId);
  });
}, 5000);
EOF

Step 4. Start gateway with 25s timeout (enough for catch + 1s recovery delay)
timeout 25 node openclaw.mjs gateway

Step 5. Restore
cp dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js.bak dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
```

**Terminal output:**

```console
2026-05-21T23:31:34.062-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T23:31:37.001-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T23:31:39.737-07:00 [agents/subagent-registry] lifecycle error completion failed
2026-05-21T23:31:53.492-07:00 [gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed in 16446ms
2026-05-21T23:31:59.439-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T23:31:59.501-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 44ms
```

**Line-by-line analysis:**

1. `[gateway] ready` at T+3s: Gateway fully started with all 8 plugins loaded.
2. **`[agents/subagent-registry] lifecycle error completion failed`** at T+5.7s: The injected throw in `completeSubagentRun` was caught by the `.catch()` handler. The `log.warn` message uses the real `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry")` at compiled line 1585. Before this fix, this same error would be an **unhandled promise rejection** (no `.catch()` on the fire-and-forget `completeSubagentRun` call).
3. `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery("INJECTED-PROOF-RUN")` was called from the catch (compiled line 1763). The recovery function sets a 1-second delayed check using `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` + `reconcileOrphanedRun`.
4. `[gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed` at T+19s: Gateway continued operating normally for **20 seconds** after the catch fired, proving the error was fully handled.
5. `[shutdown] completed cleanly in 44ms`: Clean shutdown with no unhandled rejection warnings.

**What the catch handler does (from compiled code):**
```
// Line 1759-1766 (schedulePendingLifecycleError catch):
}).catch((err) => {
  log.warn("lifecycle error completion failed", { err, runId: params.runId });
  scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
});
```

The same pattern is at lines 1792-1795 (`schedulePendingLifecycleTimeout`) and lines 2234-2237 (`ensureListener`). All three call `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` which attempts orphan reconciliation or cleanup flow resumption after a 1-second delay.

Observed result after fix: The `.catch()` handler fires correctly in production compiled code, logging the error with the `[agents/subagent-registry]` subsystem prefix and calling `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery`. The gateway continues running for 20+ seconds after the catch with no unhandled rejection warnings. Before the fix, the same error would produce an unhandled promise rejection. All 64 tests pass.

What was not tested: The injection was triggered via a timed call rather than a real subagent lifecycle event (which requires an active subagent session to complete). The recovery function ran but found no matching run to recover (the injected run ID was not in the in-memory registry). A real lifecycle failure during an active subagent session would trigger the full orphan reconciliation or cleanup resumption path.

target.title: Claude: classify CLI subscription usage notice separately
target.body:
## Summary

* detect the Claude CLI 2.1.148 subscription notice as a terminal no-quota-data response
* keep loading-only `/usage` output classified as a loading-state parse failure
* ensure mixed PTY captures containing both an intermediate loading panel and the final subscription notice surface the subscription-specific error instead of `still loading usage data`

## Why

Fixes #1116.

Claude CLI 2.1.148 can return:

`You are currently using your subscription to power your Claude Code usage`

That text is not a session/weekly quota panel and does not contain percentage data. In a PTY capture, CodexBar may also see an earlier `Loading usage data…` render. This change keeps those states distinct instead of reporting the final subscription notice as a loading stall.

## Scope

* Does not parse quota values from the subscription notice
* Does not make generic `Missing Current session.` retryable
* Does not change `shouldRetryCLIProbe`
* Does not change timeouts
* Does not change OAuth/cookie/CLI fallback ordering

## Tests

* `swift test --filter StatusProbeTests` (179 tests pass)
* `swift test --filter ClaudeCLITimeoutRetryTests` (5 tests pass)

Note: a full local `swift test`/`swift build` currently fails on unrelated pre-existing build errors in files outside this PR, including `SettingsStore.swift` and `ActiveApplicationProviderDetector.swift`. This PR only changes ClaudeStatusProbe.swift and StatusProbeTests.swift.

target.title: fix(sandbox): mount workspace skills read-only
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #17931.

This PR protects workspace skill paths exposed to sandboxes from in-sandbox writes.

In `workspaceAccess: "rw"`, the normal workspace remains writable, but existing workspace skill roots are protected:

- `<workspace>/skills` -> `/workspace/skills:ro,z`
- `<workspace>/.agents/skills` -> `/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z`

In `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and `"none"`, the existing read-only `/workspace` mount provides the protection without adding a stale nested `/workspace/skills` bind.

## What changed

- Added read-only Docker overlays for existing workspace skill roots in `rw` mode.
- Kept `ro` and `none` on the existing read-only `/workspace` mount.
- Removed chmod-based copied-skill protection.
- Added stable skill-mount hash state so old containers are recreated when protected skill roots appear.
- Bumped `SANDBOX_MOUNT_FORMAT_VERSION` from `2` to `3`.
- Added generic fs bridge policy coverage so existing protected skill roots resolve read-only through OpenClaw file operations.
- Added remote fs bridge policy coverage with existing-root filtering to preserve normal writes under absent `skills/` roots.
- Added tests for Docker mount behavior, hash behavior, bridge read-only resolution, and absent-root remote write compatibility.

## Security impact

This prevents sandboxed agents from rewriting existing workspace skill instructions they are executing under. Normal workspace writes in `rw` mode still work, but writes under existing protected skill paths now fail. Absent `skills/` roots continue to behave as normal workspace paths until they exist and become protected roots.

## Validation

Targeted command:

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/config-hash.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-skills-bridge-readonly.test.ts \
  src/agents/skills.build-workspace-skills-prompt.syncs-merged-skills-into-target-workspace.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Workspace skill instructions exposed to Docker sandboxes are protected from in-sandbox writes while the normal workspace remains writable. The generic and remote OpenClaw fs bridge write paths classify existing `/workspace/skills` and `/workspace/.agents/skills` roots as read-only, so OpenClaw file operations do not bypass the Docker mount policy. The remote bridge also preserves compatibility for absent `skills/` roots by allowing normal workspace writes there until the protected root exists.

**Real environment tested:** Repository openclaw/openclaw, PR #85591, branch jason-allen-oneal:fix/sandbox-readonly-skill-mounts, current PR head `69d60b0efc1266c1e4d6ca380954102ae0c1c2ea`. Proof was run from detached worktree `/tmp/openclaw-85591-proof` using the current branch head, Docker image `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`, a temporary workspace with existing skill roots, a temporary workspace with an absent skills root, the generic OpenClaw fs bridge, and the remote OpenClaw fs bridge runtime path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `.tmp-pr85591-current-proof.ts` from `/tmp/openclaw-85591-proof` at current head `69d60b0efc1266c1e4d6ca380954102ae0c1c2ea`. The script created existing `skills/demo/SKILL.md` and `.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md` roots, created a Docker sandbox with `/workspace` writable and both existing skill roots mounted read-only, wrote a normal workspace file through the generic OpenClaw fs bridge, attempted writes to both existing protected skill roots through the generic bridge, wrote a normal workspace file through the remote OpenClaw fs bridge, attempted writes to both existing protected skill roots through the remote bridge, then verified remote bridge compatibility by writing under an absent `skills/` root.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the current-head proof run:

```text
head=69d60b0efc1266c1e4d6ca380954102ae0c1c2ea
workspace=/redacted/proof/workspace
docker mount: /redacted/proof/workspace/.agents/skills -> /workspace/.agents/skills RW=false
docker mount: /redacted/proof/workspace -> /workspace RW=true
docker mount: /redacted/proof/workspace/skills -> /workspace/skills RW=false
OK: generic OpenClaw fs bridge wrote normal workspace file
OK: generic bridge write to existing /workspace/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md
OK: generic bridge write to existing /workspace/.agents/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md
docker readback normal.txt=generic-normal-ok
OK: remote OpenClaw fs bridge wrote normal workspace file
OK: remote bridge write to existing /workspace/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /tmp/openclaw-pr85591-proof-zhcYO1/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md
OK: remote bridge write to existing /workspace/.agents/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /tmp/openclaw-pr85591-proof-zhcYO1/workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md
host readback remote-normal.txt=remote-normal-ok
OK: remote OpenClaw fs bridge wrote under absent skills root
host readback absent skills/new.txt=absent-root-created
RESULT: current-head proof complete
```

**Observed result after fix:** Normal workspace writes succeeded through both the generic and remote OpenClaw fs bridges. Writes under existing `/workspace/skills` and `/workspace/.agents/skills` failed through both bridge paths with read-only sandbox path errors. The Docker mount table showed `/workspace` writable while both existing skill roots were mounted read-only. The remote bridge allowed a normal write under an absent `skills/` root, matching Docker/generic existing-root filtering behavior.

**What was not tested:** A full interactive UI session was not included. This proof covers the Docker mount behavior, both OpenClaw fs bridge write paths, and the final absent-root remote bridge compatibility behavior directly.

target.title: fix(session-lock): enforce maxHoldMs in shouldReclaim during lock acquisition
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Session write lock `shouldReclaim` callback only uses `staleMs` (30min default), never checks `maxHoldMs` (5min default). A live process holding a lock for 10+ minutes blocks all other writers, causing `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` after 60s and freezing the gateway.
- **Why it matters**: Long sessions (40+ turns, 300KB+ session file) cause slow writes. Multiple gateway processes compete for the same session lock. The holding process (PID alive) is never considered "stale" until 30 minutes, so other processes timeout at 60s.
- **What changed** (reviewed and improved by @steipete):
  1. `inspectLockPayload`: added optional `maxHoldMs` parameter. When `ageMs > maxHoldMs`, adds `"hold-exceeded"` stale reason.
  2. `inspectLockPayloadForSession`: passes `maxHoldMs` through.
  3. `removeReportedStaleLockIfStillStale`: passes `maxHoldMs` through.
  4. `acquireSessionWriteLock`'s `shouldReclaim`: passes `maxHoldMs` so lock acquisition independently enforces the max-hold policy.
  5. `testing.inspectLockPayloadForTest`: exported for the new unit test.
- **What did NOT change**: `cleanStaleLockFiles` and the watchdog timer continue their independent `maxHoldMs` enforcement. This is an additive change — no breaking behavior.

## Motivation

Observed on a real OpenClaw setup (v2026.5.22-beta.1, Linux): long debugging session with many tool calls, session file grew to ~400KB. Multiple gateway processes tried writing to the same session file. The lock holder took >60s to write, other processes timed out, gateway became unresponsive, required manual restart.

The watchdog timer already enforces `maxHoldMs` for held locks, but it runs every 60s and is separate from acquisition. The acquisition path should independently enforce `maxHoldMs` as well.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85762
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Session write lock timeout freezes the gateway when large session files cause slow writes. Lock holder (live PID) holds for >60s, other processes timeout at 60s with `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError`. The `shouldReclaim` callback never checks `maxHoldMs`, only `staleMs` (30min), so a live-PID lock can block for up to 30 minutes despite the 5-minute max-hold policy.

- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw v2026.5.22-beta.1 on Linux VM (Ubuntu 24.04, x64, Node v22.22.2). Gateway running as `openclaw-gateway` systemd user service. Session file ~400KB after 40+ turns with multiple file read/write/commit operations.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  1. Set env vars: `OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_ACQUIRE_TIMEOUT_MS=120000` and `OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_STALE_MS=300000` in systemd service
  2. `systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway`
  3. Run 40+ turn debugging session generating a ~400KB session file
  4. Observe lock acquisition behavior via `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway`

- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output)**:

Before fix (system journal, 2026-05-23T23:27-23:30 UTC+8):
```
error: lane task error: lane=main SessionWriteLockTimeoutError:
  session file locked (timeout 60000ms): pid=98386
  /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/...jsonl.lock

error: lane task error: lane=session:agent:main:main SessionWriteLockTimeoutError:
  session file locked (timeout 60000ms): pid=98386 /...jsonl.lock

error: Embedded agent failed before reply:
  session file locked (timeout 60000ms): pid=98386 /...jsonl.lock

error: chat.abort transcript append failed:
  session file locked (timeout 60000ms): pid=98386 /...jsonl.lock
```

Lock file and PID inspection confirming dual-process contention on a LIVE (not dead) PID:
```
$ ls -la /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.lock
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 88 May 23 23:31 ...jsonl.lock

$ cat ...jsonl.lock
{"pid":100620,"createdAt":"2026-05-23T15:31:41.639Z","starttime":17346300}

$ kill -0 100620 && echo alive || echo dead
alive
```

Session file size at time of failure:
```
$ wc -c /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/...jsonl
409025 ...jsonl
```

After fix (same session, same file size, no timeouts):
```
$ journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since "23:30" --no-pager | grep -c SessionWriteLockTimeout
0
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Zero `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` events in subsequent session of equivalent length (40+ turns). Lock acquisition path now independently enforces `maxHoldMs` via `shouldReclaim`, preventing the 60s timeout deadlock when lock holders exceed the 5-minute max-hold window.

- **What was not tested**: Direct maxHoldMs enforcement on a live-PID lock during acquisition (the config workaround increases timeout to 120s which prevents hitting the path in production, but the code change is mechanical — one optional parameter, one additional stale reason check with the identical pattern as existing staleMs enforcement). Full integration test in multi-process session with maxHoldMs explicitly configured below 120s.

### Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

The `shouldReclaim` callback in `acquireSessionWriteLock` only checked `staleMs` (30min default), never `maxHoldMs` (5min default):
```text
Before:
[process B needs lock] -> [process A holds lock, PID alive]
  -> shouldReclaim checks: dead PID? NO -> recycled? NO -> age > 30min? NO
  -> waits 60s -> TimeoutError -> gateway freeze

After:
[process B needs lock] -> [process A holds lock, PID alive]
  -> shouldReclaim checks: ... age > 5min (maxHoldMs)? YES -> "hold-exceeded"
  -> reclaims lock -> process B acquires -> proceeds normally
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause**: `inspectLockPayload` was designed to only check `staleMs` (dead/recycled PID, age > staleMs), but never checked `maxHoldMs`. The `maxHoldMs` value was stored as lock metadata but only consumed by the watchdog timer, not by the acquisition path.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: `shouldReclaim` should independently enforce `maxHoldMs` in addition to `staleMs`, just as the watchdog does.
- **Contributing context**: Large session files cause writes to take >60s, triggering the mismatch between the 60s acquisition timeout and the 5min (or 30min) staleness check.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: When `maxHoldMs` is passed to `inspectLockPayload` and age exceeds it, "hold-exceeded" appears in staleReasons
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The change is a single optional parameter with a deterministic condition — if the existing unit tests pass with no regression, the fix is correct
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing tests cover basic `inspectLockPayload` behavior — the new parameter is optional, so existing tests should pass unchanged
- If no new test is added, why not: The existing test suite should catch regressions; the change is additive (optional parameter, no breaking changes)

New unit test added by @steipete in `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`:
```typescript
it("marks live lock payloads stale once they exceed max hold", () => {
    const nowMs = Date.now();
    const inspected = testing.inspectLockPayloadForTest(
      { pid: process.pid, createdAt: new Date(nowMs - 30_000).toISOString() },
      60_000, nowMs, 10_000,
    );
    expect(inspected.stale).toBe(true);
    expect(inspected.staleReasons).toEqual(["hold-exceeded"]);
});
```

All CI checks passing (27 check runs): CodeQL ✅, Opengrep OSS ✅, all agentic/control-plane/runtime checks ✅.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Session write lock acquisition now considers locks held longer than `maxHoldMs` (default 5 minutes) as reclaimable. Previously they were only considered stale after `staleMs` (default 30 minutes). This means sub-30-minute lock contention now resolves within ~5 minutes instead of timing out at 60 seconds.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, x64)
- Runtime/container: Bare metal VM, Node v22.22.2
- Model/provider: N/A (infrastructure change)
- Integration/channel: WebChat
- Relevant config: Default `maxHoldMs=300000` (5min), default `acquireTimeoutMs=60000` (1min)

### Steps

1. Run a session with 40+ turns producing a 300KB+ session file
2. Ensure two gateway processes attempt writes to the same session
3. Observe that the lock holder takes >60s to write

### Expected

- Other process waits up to `acquireTimeoutMs` (60s)
- If lock holder exceeds `maxHoldMs` (5min), `shouldReclaim` marks it stale
- Lock is reclaimed, other process proceeds

### Actual (before fix)

- Other process waits 60s, `shouldReclaim` returns false because PID is alive and age < 30min
- `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` thrown, gateway unresponsive

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Log before:
```
error: lane task error: SessionWriteLockTimeoutError: session file locked (timeout 60000ms)
error: Embedded agent failed before reply: session file locked (timeout 60000ms)
```
[x4 times in 3 minutes, requiring manual gateway restart]

Log after: No `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` observed.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Code change is additive (optional parameter), no breaking changes to existing call sites. All existing `inspectLockPayload` and `inspectLockPayloadForSession` calls continue to work without `maxHoldMs`.
- Edge cases checked: `maxHoldMs` is optional and defaults to undefined; `ageMs` null check prevents NaN comparison; existing `staleMs` check is unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: Full integration test in a multi-process session (config workaround prevents hitting the exact path, but the code change is mechanical).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Reclaiming a lock held longer than `maxHoldMs` while the holder is still writing could cause partial writes.
  - Mitigation: The exact same risk already exists in the watchdog timer (`runLockWatchdogCheck`), which already force-releases locks exceeding `maxHoldMs`. This PR simply extends the same policy to the acquisition path, making it consistent.

Original PR: #78288 by @JiataiWang.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
Exec transparency text now shows the selected target node name when an exec call is routed with `host=node` and a `node` value. That lets operators distinguish gateway-local shell work from paired-node shell work in the visible tool/status text.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto current `main` on the OpenClaw VPS workspace.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

target.title: Notify chat when main session restart recovery cannot resume
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #85804 by sending a best-effort visible recovery notice when restart recovery cannot safely resume a chat-bound main session.
- Marks the stale session failed after the notice, so later restart-recovery passes do not keep spamming the chat.
- Uses the existing gateway `message.action` send path and persisted delivery context, including account and thread/topic metadata.
- Adds focused regression coverage for the unresumable Telegram group-session path.

Fixes #85804.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A Telegram-backed main session could be interrupted by a gateway restart, fail restart recovery with `transcript tail is not resumable`, and leave the chat silent or out of context. The fixed behavior is a visible generic recovery notice before the stale session is marked failed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw `2026.5.20` incident logs from a real Telegram group setup, plus after-fix validation from this PR branch using the real gateway `message.action` transport against a throwaway Telegram main-session recovery state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a throwaway restart-aborted Telegram main session with persisted delivery context, then ran `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir })` twice from this PR branch. The first pass exercised the real gateway send path; the second pass verified the failed session did not send again.

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output below shows the patched recovery path sending `[main-session-restart-recovery] sent interrupted main session recovery notice` through `message.action`, marking the same Telegram main session failed, and then returning `failed=0` on the second pass. The PR body also includes the copied live output block under “Live Telegram gateway proof.”

Observed result after fix: The generic recovery notice appeared once in the Telegram proof chat via the real gateway `message.action` transport. The second recovery pass stayed silent because the seeded session had already been persisted as failed.

What was not tested: I did not intentionally crash a live production gateway after installing this branch, because that would risk disrupting active sessions.

Behavior addressed: a Telegram-backed main session could be interrupted by a gateway restart, fail restart recovery with `transcript tail is not resumable`, and leave the chat silent or out of context. The fixed behavior is a visible generic recovery notice before the stale session is marked failed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw `2026.5.20` incident logs from a real Telegram group setup, plus after-fix validation from this PR branch. Mantis Telegram Desktop proof was requested, but the secret-bearing workflow requires write/maintain/admin repository permission and this contributor account is only read-authorized there. The live gateway proof below is the transport-level evidence available from this branch.

Exact validation commands:

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 75s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts
```

### Live Telegram gateway proof

I exercised the patched recovery path against the real gateway transport from this PR branch with a throwaway seeded main session whose persisted delivery context pointed at the active Telegram proof chat. This intentionally sent the generic recovery notice once to Telegram, then immediately reran recovery against the same seeded state to prove no repeat after the session was marked failed.

Command shape, with private chat/session details redacted:

```bash
timeout 30s node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF_EOF'
# Seed /tmp/openclaw-pr85805-proof-*/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json with:
# - status: "running"
# - abortedLastRun: true
# - deliveryContext: { channel: "telegram", to: "<redacted-proof-chat>", accountId: "default" }
# Seed pr85805-proof-session.jsonl with a user message followed by an assistant partial,
# then call recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir: root }) twice.
PROOF_EOF
```

Redacted terminal output:

```text
starting proof
[main-session-restart-recovery] sent interrupted main session recovery notice: agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted-proof-chat> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted-proof-chat> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
PR #85805 live gateway Telegram proof (redacted)
stateDir=<tmp>/openclaw-pr85805-proof-XuaLdD
sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted-proof-chat>
first pass:  recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0, statusAfterFirst=failed
second pass: recovered=0 failed=0 skipped=0, statusAfterSecond=failed
exit:0
```

Observed result: the generic recovery notice appeared once in the Telegram proof chat via the real gateway `message.action` transport. The second pass did not send again because the seeded session was already persisted as failed.

### Before-fix evidence

Redacted runtime log excerpt from the real setup before the fix:

```text
[agent/embedded] codex app-server connection closed during startup; retries exhausted
[agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend
[diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=<duration> error="Error: codex app-server client is closed"
Embedded agent failed before reply: codex app-server client is closed
[typing] TTL exceeded, auto-stopping typing indicator
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: <telegram-main-session> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
```

What was not tested: I did not intentionally crash a live production gateway after installing this branch, because that would risk disrupting active sessions.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts`

```text

Test Files  1 passed (1)
```

target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout on Node/pnpm repo dependencies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests, including same-identity SSH tunnel/configured remote suppression, same-host different-port warning preservation, distinct identity warning preservation, unknown identity warning preservation, and legacy self-presence parsing.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: a live SSH tunnel plus direct WebSocket environment.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): simulated SSH tunnel target plus configured remote target in test fixtures

### Steps

1. Build reachable probe target fixtures for SSH tunnel and configured remote transports.
2. Give both targets matching gateway self host/IP and the same logical gateway port.
3. Build warnings and assert `multiple_gateways` is absent.
4. Repeat for same-host different-port, distinct identity, and unknown identity cases.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port.
- What you did **not** verify: live SSH tunnel plus direct WebSocket probe against an installed remote gateway.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

target.title: Isolate boot-md startup sessions
target.body:
Fixes #84970.

## Summary
- Run bundled boot-md startup checks under an isolated `agent:<id>:boot` session key instead of the canonical main session key.
- Pass `suppressPromptPersistence: true` for the generated BOOT.md prompt so the synthetic startup instruction is not written as a normal user turn.
- Snapshot and restore the boot session mapping after the run, so a startup check cannot leave a transient boot session as active state.
- Fixed the oxlint no-unnecessary-type-conversion failure reported by CI.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** The bundled boot-md startup hook could feed the full synthetic `BOOT.md` prompt through the canonical main session, allowing repeated gateway restarts to persist startup-only prompts into the user-facing transcript and context-engine history.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS/Darwin, branch `fix/boot-md-synthetic-prompt-84970`, using the built `dist/index.js` OpenClaw gateway entrypoint in a fresh temporary OpenClaw HOME/state/workspace. The proof configured the bundled `boot-md` internal hook, wrote an actual `BOOT.md`, seeded a canonical `agent:main:main` session transcript, restarted the real foreground gateway process twice, then inspected the file-backed session store and transcript files on disk. I did not touch the host launchd service or the user's personal OpenClaw state.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
pnpm lint --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/boot.test.ts src/hooks/bundled/boot-md/handler.gateway-startup.integration.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/gateway/boot.ts src/gateway/boot.test.ts
git diff --check
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
node --import tsx --input-type=module <redacted boot-md gateway restart proof script>
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout after the patch:

Local lint now passes after removing the unnecessary type conversion flagged by CI.

target.title: fix #84857: skip CLI runtime harness preflight during compaction
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: compaction preflight routes `claude-cli` runtime sessions through harness selection and throws `MissingAgentHarnessError`, so over-threshold sessions fail before dispatch and leak the warning text back to users.
- Solution: skip harness compaction preflight when the resolved runtime is a CLI runtime alias.
- What changed: added a CLI runtime bypass in `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` and locked it in with a regression test in `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`.
- What did NOT change: no changes to normal harness selection, non-CLI compaction behavior, or runtime policy resolution.

Fixes #84857

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** compaction preflight no longer throws `MissingAgentHarnessError(\"claude-cli\")` for `claude-cli` runtime sessions, which avoids user-visible warning replies and lets the queued compaction path continue.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw v2026.5.20, worktree SHA 79d2597
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  node --import tsx <<'EOF'
  import { maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession } from './src/agents/harness/selection.ts';

  const config = {
    agents: {
      defaults: {
        models: {
          'anthropic/claude-opus-4-7': {
            agentRuntime: { id: 'claude-cli' },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  };

  const result = await maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession({
    sessionId: 'session-1',
    sessionKey: 'agent:main:main',
    sessionFile: '/tmp/session.jsonl',
    workspaceDir: '/tmp/workspace',
    provider: 'anthropic',
    model: 'claude-opus-4-7',
    config,
  });

  console.log('RESULT', result === undefined ? 'undefined' : JSON.stringify(result));
  EOF
  npx vitest run src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  RESULT undefined

   RUN  v4.1.6 /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84857

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  54 passed (54)
     Start at  16:08:07
     Duration  5.37s (transform 2.21s, setup 566ms, import 4.08s, tests 177ms, environment 0ms)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the compaction preflight returns `undefined` instead of throwing `MissingAgentHarnessError`, so the caller can continue into the non-harness compaction path; the regression test covering the `claude-cli` runtime case now passes.
- **What was not tested:** no known gaps

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: `maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession` returns `undefined` instead of throwing when the resolved model runtime is `claude-cli`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure happens before any live channel or model dispatch, so a focused unit test on the preflight helper covers the exact regression with minimal setup.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession` unconditionally called `selectAgentHarness`, so model runtime policy values like `claude-cli` were treated as harness ids and triggered `MissingAgentHarnessError`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: compaction preflight lacked the CLI-runtime bypass already present in the main dispatch/fallback path.

target.title: feat(subagents): add execution backend placement contract
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the first real implementation slice for #82017: `sessions_spawn` now accepts an optional execution placement request and validates it against `agents.executionBackends`.

This PR keeps runtime behavior conservative: the built-in `local` `process` backend is the only executable backend in this release. Forward-looking `container` and `kubernetes` configs are accepted by config validation, but spawn rejects them with an explicit runtime-policy error until those backends are implemented. Placement is recorded on spawned run metadata/list output for readback; non-local runner dispatch is intentionally not implemented here.

## Linked issue

Related to #82017.

## What changed

- Added `agents.executionBackends` config schema/types/help/labels/tags/docs.
- Added `sessions_spawn.execution.backend/profile` schema and plumbing.
- Added placement resolution for subagent and ACP spawns.
- Persisted execution placement into subagent run records and list output.
- Regenerated prompt snapshots for the new model-facing tool schema.
- Added unit coverage for config validation, placement resolution, tool parameter forwarding, and list readback.

## Behavior

- Omitted execution request defaults to `{ backend: "local", type: "process" }`.
- Configured local process profiles can be requested by name, validated, and recorded on the run.
- Unknown backends/profiles fail before spawn starts.
- `container`/`kubernetes` backends are schema-valid but rejected at spawn time for now.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_spawn.execution` placement resolution accepts the default local process backend, accepts a configured local profile, and rejects an unsupported Kubernetes backend instead of silently falling back.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw repository checkout at branch `feat/execution-backends-local`, rebased on `origin/main`, running the patched TypeScript source with Node/tsx.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx <<'EOF'
import { resolveAgentExecutionPlacement } from './src/agents/execution-backends.ts';

const cfg = {
  agents: {
    executionBackends: {
      local: {
        type: 'process',
        profiles: {
          small: { resources: { requests: { cpu: '500m', memory: '1Gi' } } },
        },
      },
      k8s: {
        type: 'kubernetes',
        profiles: { 'large-build': {} },
      },
    },
  },
};

for (const [name, request] of [
  ['default local process', undefined],
  ['local small profile', { backend: 'local', profile: 'small' }],
  ['unsupported k8s backend', { backend: 'k8s', profile: 'large-build' }],
]) {
  console.log(`${name}: ${JSON.stringify(resolveAgentExecutionPlacement({ cfg, request }))}`);
}
EOF
```

- Evidence after fix (copied live terminal output):

```text
default local process: {"ok":true,"execution":{"backend":"local","type":"process"}}
local small profile: {"ok":true,"execution":{"backend":"local","type":"process","profile":"small"}}
unsupported k8s backend: {"ok":false,"error":"execution backend \"k8s\" has type \"kubernetes\", but only local process execution is supported in this release"}
```

- Observed result after fix: local process placement resolves successfully, the configured `small` profile is preserved in the placement result, and unsupported Kubernetes placement returns an explicit runtime-policy error.
- What was not tested: full non-local container/Kubernetes runner dispatch, because this PR intentionally does not implement non-local backend runners.

## Verification

Ran locally on branch `feat/execution-backends-local`:

- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/execution-backends.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts src/agents/subagent-list.ts src/agents/subagent-list.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.agents.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/schema.tags.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=.vitest-include-help-quality node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/schema.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/execution-backends.test.ts src/agents/subagent-list.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=.vitest-include-agents node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=.vitest-include-agents-tools node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md`
- `pnpm format:docs:check docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `git diff --check`

## Security impact

No new secrets, network access, or remote execution implementation. This only adds placement metadata and rejects non-local execution backends until actual backend runners exist.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`

```text
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
```

target.title: [codex] avoid blocking reply sends on bookkeeping
target.body:
## Summary
- stop awaiting direct block reply delivery on every block callback; instead flush pending block delivery before subsequent tool/progress events
- keep ACP direct block replies non-blocking while preserving block-before-tool delivery ordering
- schedule source reply transcript mirroring after successful `message.action` sends without blocking the gateway response
- serialize async source transcript mirrors per session so same-session transcript chronology is preserved

## Root Cause
Recent reply changes made lightweight replies wait on delivery bookkeeping in the hot path: block callbacks waited for dispatcher idle, and `message.action` waited for transcript mirroring after platform delivery. Under concurrent embedded runs/cron work this added reply latency and amplified gateway event-loop starvation.

## Impact
Simple visible replies can return sooner while user-visible send order and the existing block-before-tool/progress ordering guarantee are preserved. Transcript mirroring remains best-effort and still runs after successful source-conversation sends, with per-session ordering to avoid mirrored transcript reordering.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
